title: Daily Market Digest: March 18, 2026
subtitle: Market sentiment cautiously optimistic ahead of Fed decision; oil prices ease slightly

items.headline: US Stock Futures Climb as Investors Await Fed Decision
items.summary: US equity futures are trading higher this morning as the market anticipates the Federal Reserve's interest rate decision later today. Crude oil prices have eased slightly due to news of resumed exports via Turkey, although geopolitical tensions in the Middle East continue to influence sentiment.

sections.title: Top Movers (US Market - Previous Day's Extended Trading)

items.headline: DocuSign Rises on Strong Earnings
items.summary: DocuSign shares rose 1.5% in extended trading on March 17 following stronger-than-expected earnings results.

items.headline: Lululemon Declines After Disappointing Results
items.summary: Lululemon shares declined over 1% in extended trading on March 17, reacting to disappointing quarterly results.

items.headline: Oklo Falls on Weak Quarterly Performance
items.summary: Oklo saw its stock fall more than 2% in extended trading on March 17, attributed to disappointing quarterly results.

items.headline: Federal Reserve's Policy Meeting and Powell's Commentary in Focus
items.summary: Investors are keenly awaiting the outcome of the US Federal Reserve's policy meeting later today, March 18. While the central bank is widely expected to keep interest rates steady in the 3.5%-3.75% range, guidance from Fed Chair Jerome Powell regarding the influence of surging oil prices on future policy and inflation will be closely scrutinized.

items.headline: Crude Oil Prices Ease Amid Export Deal, Geopolitical Risks Persist
items.summary: Crude oil prices have seen a decline on March 18, with Brent crude trading around $101.13 a barrel (down 2.23%) and WTI at $93.19 a barrel (down 3.14%), primarily due to reports of rising US inventories and a deal to resume exports via Turkey's Ceyhan port. However, Brent crude remains above $100 as risks from the Iran conflict continue to loom.

items.headline: Energy, Consumer Discretionary, and Communication Services Lead S&P Sectors
items.summary: As of March 17's close, eight of the 11 S&P sectors closed higher, with Energy, Consumer Discretionary, and Communication Services leading the gains. Conversely, consumer staples and healthcare sectors experienced declines. In the Indian market, Nifty IT jumped over 4%, while the Nifty Metal sector lagged.

items.headline: Key Earnings Reports Expected Today: Micron Technology, Five Below, and Lufax
items.summary: Several major companies are scheduled to release their quarterly earnings reports after market hours on Wednesday, March 18. Micron Technology ($MU) and Five Below ($FIVE) are among those expected to report, with investors watching for their performance. Lufax ($LU) also announced earnings today.
